name: climber
comment: Hold the handlebars. Step one foot on the lower foot pedal and the other foot on the higher foot pedal, making both feet level with each other. Adjust the handles to chin height. Turn the machine on. Push one foot pedal down and pull down the same-side handlebar, allowing the opposite foot pedal and handlebar to rise. Alternate the movement on the opposite side. Repeat.

name: ladder mill
comment: Secure the belt cable attachment around the waist with the cable at the back. Climb onto the ladder by stepping onto a rung near the bottom and grasp a rung closer to the middle. Climb by stepping up and grasping one rung at a time. Intensity can be increased by climbing faster or higher on the ladder, as greater cable tension allows the rungs to travel downward at a greater speed. A weighted vest can also be worn to increase difficulty. To stop, climb more slowly lower down on the rungs, then step down to the ground.

name: bicycling
comment: Push the pedal with the power foot by extending the hip and knee to propel the bike forward, while the opposite recovery foot maintains contact with the other pedal. Once the recovery foot is in position, push the pedal with the opposite foot to continue the cycle. Repeat this sequence of power and recovery, alternating between sides to keep the bike in motion whilst maintaining balance.

name: cycle ergometer
comment: Sit on the upright cycle seat with the feet on the pedals and grasp the handlebars. Select 'Quick Start' or enter a programme. Pedal.

name: recumbent cycle
comment: Sit on the seat and grasp the handlebars at the sides. Place the feet on the pedals. Select 'Quick Start' or enter a programme. Pedal.

name: elliptical cross trainer
comment: Step onto the pedal platforms and grasp the handlebars on each arm lever. Select 'Quick Start' or enter a programme. Pedal forward whilst alternating the arm levers back and forth.

name: elliptical trainer
comment: Step onto the pedal platforms and grasp the handle rails. Select 'Quick Start' or enter a programme. Pedal forward by placing weight on one foot, allowing the other foot to move forward. Alternate with the opposite side until momentum allows continued forward rotation.

name: rider
comment: Sit on the seat. Place the feet on the pedals and grasp the handlebars. Extend the hips and knees whilst pulling the handlebars back to the sides. Return to the starting position. Repeat.

name: row ergometer
comment: Sit on the seat. Position and secure the feet on the platform. Grasp the handlebar with both hands in an overhand grip. With the legs extended, pull the handlebar to the midsection with the forearms horizontal. Extend the arms and lean forward slightly from the hips. Slide forward by flexing the legs until the shins are vertical and the body is pressed up to the legs. Slide back by extending the legs whilst keeping the arms straight. As the knees straighten, pull the handlebar back to the midsection and lean back slightly. Repeat.

name: ski machine
comment: Step onto the pedal platforms and grasp a rope grip in each hand. Kick and glide the pedal platforms back and forth whilst swinging the arms along the sides. Swing the arms so constant tension is maintained on the arm swinging down, extending the forward arm as far as is comfortable.

name: ski ergometer
comment: Place the wrists through the straps, up through the undersides of the loops, then grasp the handles with each hand. Stand with a shoulder-width stance with the arms bent in front and the hands slightly above the head. Pull both cable handles downward by extending the shoulders, flexing the spine, and bending the legs. Extend the arms downward and slightly back at the bottom of the stroke. Return to the starting position by extending the body and arms upward. Repeat.

name: stair climber
comment: Step onto the pedal platforms and grasp the handrails. Select 'Quick Start' or enter a programme. Place weight on one pedal and allow the other pedal to rise. Before the lower pedal hits the bottom or the upper pedal hits the top, alternate with the opposite movement. Repeat.

name: stepmill
comment: Step onto the stairs and grasp the handrails. Select 'Quick Start' or enter a programme. Climb the stairs to keep pace with the machine.

name: sprint machine
comment: Straddle the machine facing the handlebars with the levers behind. Grasp the handles and place the feet on the pedals within the foot holsters. Extend the arms straight to support the torso. Push one pedal down by extending the leg whilst pulling the other pedal up by flexing the hip and knee of the opposite leg. Move the levers in the opposite direction, alternating pushing and pulling movements.

name: treadmill run
comment: Step onto the deck of the treadmill and grasp the rails. Straddle the belt by standing on the sides of the treadmill. Attach the safety tether. Select 'Quick Start' or enter a programme. Run, keeping pace with the treadmill. Maintain a close distance from the console. A hand can be placed on the rail to regain balance. Avoid transferring body weight or hanging onto the handlebars. Intensity can be altered by incline and speed.

name: treadmill walk
comment: Step onto the deck of the treadmill and grasp the rails. Straddle the belt by standing on the sides of the treadmill. Attach the safety tether. Select 'Quick Start' or enter a programme. Walk, keeping pace with the treadmill. Maintain a close distance from the console. A hand can be placed on the rail for balance. Avoid transferring body weight or hanging onto the handlebars. Intensity can be altered by incline and speed. Spinal rotation occurs during brisk walking.

name: self-propelled treadmill walk
comment: Step onto the side cover of the treadmill with a hand on the side rail. Straddle the belt by standing on the side covers with a hand on the rails at each side. Stand in the middle of the treadmill facing the console. Step forward and release the handrails. Increase speed and frequency by stepping slightly further up on the tread until the desired speed is reached. Maintain speed by moderating stride length and speed of strides in an upright posture. A hand can be placed on the rail for balance. Avoid transferring body weight or hanging onto the handlebars. Self-propelled treadmills allow a change of speed at will without having to push console buttons. To accelerate, take longer steps, speed up, and step forward beyond the centre of the treadmill. To slow down, take shorter steps, slow down, and keep the feet closer to the centre of the treadmill with the body upright. To stop, simply stop walking. Spinal rotation occurs during brisk walking.

name: self-propelled treadmill run
comment: Step onto the side cover of the treadmill with a hand on the side rail. Straddle the belt by standing on the side covers with a hand on the rails at each side. Stand in the middle of the treadmill facing the console. Step forward and release the handrails. Continue to take longer and more frequent steps beyond the centre of the treadmill to accelerate. Increase speed and frequency by stepping slightly further up on the tread and begin running. Maintain speed by moderating stride length and speed of strides. Hands can be placed on the rail to maintain or regain balance. Avoid transferring body weight or hanging onto the handlebars. Self-propelled treadmills allow a change of speed at will without having to push console buttons. To accelerate, take longer steps, speed up, and step forward beyond the centre of the treadmill. To slow down, keep the body upright, take shorter steps, slow down, and keep strides closer to the centre of the treadmill. To stop, continue to slow down to walking speed before stopping. If a very sudden stop is required, grasp the handlebars at each side, quickly lift the body, and remove the feet from the treadmill surface to stand on the side covers.

name: treadmill sled pull
comment: Step onto the side cover of the treadmill with the hands on the handrails at each side for balance. Step onto the tread belt with both feet. Walk forward until the body is in contact with the harness belt. Adjust the harness belt if required so it is firmly positioned against the upper hips or lower waist. Adjust the sled and speed brakes to lower resistance settings. Lean forward into the harness belt whilst walking with the arms swinging at the sides. Increase speed by walking faster until the desired speed is reached. Adjust the speed brake to the desired intensity if needed. Maintain speed by moderating stride length and speed of strides. This setup allows for warm-up for simulated parachute or sled pulls. Walking speeds under low resistances can be used as a warm-up before performing runs, sprints, or pulls with greater resistances. Sled resistance provides approximately twice the resistance to the tread belt than speed resistance. Set sled resistance to the lowest setting and speed resistance to the desired intensity. If the harness is not set up, pull the harness hanging from the front of the treadmill and attach the straps to each end of the rear vertical bars under the back of the handrails. Adjust the height of the front anchor to approximately waist height so the belt of the harness is at upper hip height.

name: treadmill sled push
comment: Step onto the side cover of the treadmill with a hand on the side rail. Facing the console, straddle the belt by standing on the side covers with a hand on the rails at each side. Place the hands on the upper handles with the body angled forward and the feet staggered. Walk forward whilst pushing the handles. Under greater resistances, the hands can be placed on the lower bars with the body leaning forward more. Self-propelled treadmills allow starting, stopping, or changing speed at will without having to push console buttons. To accelerate, take longer steps, speed up, and step forward beyond the centre of the treadmill. To slow down, take shorter steps, slow down, and keep the feet closer to the centre of the treadmill with the body upright. To stop, simply stop walking.
